title: Now that were on road to the end
display_title: Now that were on road to the end
wikitext:
[]: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by Tajai » Sat Oct 17, 2020 4:51 pm Now that we're on the road to the end of Evangelion again, what are your final passing words about this franchise, that has now been existing for 25 years?

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by Derantor » Sat Oct 17, 2020 5:24 pm I can not give my final passing words on something that hasn't ended yet. I don't think anybody else can either.

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 17, 2020 8:02 pm I first heard of it on some anime website where I distinctly remember a line like "You might have watched +16 anime already but with this one you should wait till you're actually 16" I was interested because of the reputation but also because I'd seen Nadia before and I was very impressed by how it didn't treat the audience like idiots, like, at some point the little girls's parents die and they just flat out tell her that she's not going to see them again, no feelgoosy crap at all, I hadn't seen that before. Needless to say I'm really really glad I didn't wait; Even if it might be an exaggeration to say that there might not have been any 16 y/o old me (though there were defs times where it felt like that), It was an immense aesthetic joy, a horizon-broadening and an immediate attitude adjustment Sure some of the jokes got old and I'm sort of over the epistemological bits of existentialism these days, but it was there when I needed it Also in this landscape of tired soulless cashgrab sequels & remakes this one show actually managed to produce a continuation that had ppl at the edge of their seats, dissecting everything, moved to great emotion which is refreshing to say the least. I didn't expect to get to spend any more time with these characters so I'm super grateful that I'll get to visit them one last time in January even if it's probably gonna eviscerate my emotions

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Oct 24, 2020 5:41 am Its a great franchise to get into when you are 16 because your mind is at its most vulnerable and I'm glad I was able to enjoy the manga, rebuild and series during that time. I remember after 2.22 I was thinking maybe a few more years for this rebuild to finish and then I'll be an adult (wasn't too sure how that was going to turn out). Then time when by pretty quickly, I did some things, went to university, maybe matured a little, and 4.44 was always there in the background. Evangelion didn't parallel my life but I did consider its themes and even its Chirstianity symbols as a very familiar token to who I was whilst growing up. I wouldn't take it as seriously as like my Christian faith but it is there at different points of life. 'Preserverance must finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.' — James 1.4

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by A Renegade Deserter » Sat Oct 24, 2020 8:55 am I would still somewhat find it too early to say something for a farewell to Eva without having seen 3.0+1.0 and that I would still be willing to watch more new Eva made by others than Anno. NGE and Rebuild were always only Shinjis and so Annos story. A story has a beginning and end and there is no need to tell it further for being done and finished as it is. Every new Eva needs new characters with new stories that still touch on the fundamentals of what Eva is about. In these years since I found and watched Eva around 3.0s release, it became only truer and less surprising for me how it leads to where things for 3.0+1.0 stand now. It was long enough time to have moved on in life and with every hardship I faced and prevailed and every person I met and became a good friend or someone to talk to. I shoud know already what person I am and that I cling on being true to appreciate what I gained and experienced in life and how much Eva and that I had this strong patience in waiting for 3.0+1.0 reflects that.

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Mon Oct 26, 2020 10:31 am I don't see this as the end as I think there will be many new spin off manga's once Rebuild wraps up, as the original series had Angelic Days, Shinji Ikari Raising Project and Campus Apocalypse the Anima as well as The Shinji Ikari Detective Diary and Petite Eva. As I think Anno's huge mistake of not showing what happened in the time jump means that someone could do the whole rebuild as a manga but with the missing fourteen-year filler, I heard talk that after Anno is done he may also make the franchise public domain meaning other directors in Japan can make new series based on it.

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Oct 26, 2020 10:54 am silvermoonlight wrote: As I think Anno's huge mistake of not showing what happened in the time jump means that someone could do the whole rebuild as a manga but with the missing fourteen-year filler, I heard talk that after Anno is done he may also make the franchise public domain meaning other directors in Japan can make new series based on it.

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Mon Oct 26, 2020 10:59 am Zusuchan wrote: No, he won't. He has explicitly said that there will be more Eva and that these new Eva works will be made by other directors than him with their own unique sensibilities-but all of those works will be a part of Khara's output and according to an interview I can't seem to find the link to but distinctly remember, even identity. So Eva going into public domain is definitely not one of Anno's plans right now.

[]: Re: Now that we're on road to the end...             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Oct 26, 2020 11:12 am Always glad to be of help!
